archetype: Automated Volcanic Ash Cloud Forecasting and Airspace Management System for Global Air Traffic Control
domain: Volcanic Ash Cloud Monitoring and Air Traffic Control
description: This system forecasts volcanic ash cloud trajectories and provides real-time airspace management recommendations to air traffic controllers, ensuring safe flight routing and minimizing disruptions. It integrates data from satellite imagery, weather models, and ground-based sensors to predict ash cloud dispersion. The system must meet strict compliance standards for aviation safety and accuracy.
rules: MUST integrate with the Volcanic Ash Advisory Center (VAAC) protocol for ash cloud reporting
MUST comply with International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO) standards for airspace management
MUST incorporate data from multiple satellite systems, including GOES and Meteosat
MUST account for wind patterns and atmospheric conditions using Weather Research and Forecasting (WRF) models
MUST provide real-time updates to air traffic control systems, such as the Air Traffic Control System Command Center (ATCSCC)
anti_patterns: DO NOT rely solely on historical ash cloud data, as volcanic eruptions can be highly unpredictable
DO NOT neglect to account for the impact of ash clouds on aircraft engines and safety
DO NOT overlook the need for high-performance computing to handle large datasets and complex simulations
key_technical_challenges: Developing accurate ash cloud forecasting models that account for complex atmospheric conditions
Integrating data from multiple sources, including satellite imagery, ground-based sensors, and weather models
Providing real-time updates to air traffic control systems while meeting strict safety and accuracy standards

recommended_stack.language: Python, due to its extensive libraries for scientific computing and data analysis
recommended_stack.framework: Django, for its robust support for real-time data processing and integration with external systems
recommended_stack.database_engine: PostgreSQL, for its ability to handle large spatial datasets and support for advanced querying
recommended_stack.cache: Redis, for its high-performance caching capabilities
recommended_stack.message_broker: Apache Kafka, for its fault-tolerant and scalable message handling
special_services: NASA's Land, Atmosphere Near real-time Capability for EOS (LANCE) API
European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF) API
